transcript.clean_text: stage seven of the tour de swedes took the riders over big mountain passes again and abroad starting in ticino in the south of switzerland heading over the giant luchmannier pass into german-speaking and romance territory before going crossing the border into vaduz and up to malbun both in the territory belonging to liechtenstein a micro state between switzerland and austria is where the tour de swiss finishes this year stage seven the final road stage 194.6 kilometers ahead of the final day time trial only 84 riders started but the coronavirus crisis calmed down thankfully three non-starters on the day and nineteen riders got into the breakaway on the luke moneer pass after a 50 kilometer first hour coming up to the end of the race it would be israel premier tech controlling for the leader alongside ineos grenadiers who had garrite thomas in second place michael matthews was doing his thing in the intermediate sprints and he'd claim the points jersey after two sprint wins onto the final climb of the day jonas aguirre went away alexey ludsenko been in the break with him tibor pino another member of that 90-man breakaway was chasing and third on the road pino will be working his way up the mountain not going into the red behind remko even a pool decided to have a little move off the front and that was shot down by the leader jacob fulson the tenko was left by pinot and isagir caught and then immediately dropped by the frenchman two kilometers to go tibopino after having waited three years for any victory and cycling was looking to make it two in three months stefan kung had held on for a long time he'd be distanced and when danny martinez's turn finished off went sergio aguita third place at the start of the day 10 seconds away from the lead and riding into the yellow jersey garrett thomas made fulson do the chasing and after five hours five minutes of racing igita was riding away running to the finish line at the top of the climb to malbun just two kilometers from the austrian border it was tibor pino who won in austria for the first time in three years in his career just a couple of months back at the tour of the alps this time winning for the second time in his career on the tour de swiss and winning on the world tour get right thomas attacked jakob fulsang in the final 500 meters oscar rodriguez rode stealthily to finish second thomas was gaining onigita but higita would take 11 seconds on geraint thomas arigita would be the new leader by only two seconds going into the final time trough jacob fulsang would drop to 19 seconds down in third place the yellow jersey ceasing to be so at the end of a tough mountain stage all the glory going to peanut win number 33 in his career one of the best 25 seconds ahead of rodriguez 38 in front of ludsenko here guitar pulling on a leaders jersey for the first time since he won the tour of catalonia in march and jakob fulsangus looks so good all week still in with a shout but it's igita in yellow thomas the strongest time trialist on paper and perhaps the favorite to win the tour de swiss as the riders take on the 25 kilometer route in vaduz starting on sunday so two seconds and 19 seconds to the podium palace is still fourth ports of evo is up to fifth kung is in seventh this is what stage eight looks like then beginning an ending in the capital of liechtenstein vaduz it's fairly flat a couple of little lumps and bumps here and there the rhine river on one side of the course coming back into the capital after those 25 kilometers it'll be the final stage and the deciding stage in this year's tour de swiss the 85th edition of the race and you'll be able to watch it live and on demand on gcn plus and discovery plus you
